moves.name: Ask the Oracle
moves.triggerShort: When you seek to resolve uncertainty or determine what happens next.
moves.trigger:
When you seek to resolve uncertainty, introduce something new, or determine what happens next while playing solo or co-op, you may…
  • Envision the answer based on the situation and your intuition.
  • Roll on an oracle table (Action + Theme, Pay the Price, or a thematic table) to generate an answer.
  • Ask a yes/no question and consult the following table.
moves.notes: Your most important solo and co-op tool. Interpret results through the lens of the current situation. When the answer is obvious from context, don't roll — just narrate. Embrace surprising results as gifts; they push the story in unexpected directions.

moves.name: Pay the Price
moves.triggerShort: When you suffer the outcome of a move.
moves.trigger:
When you suffer the outcome of a move, choose one.
  • Make the most obvious negative outcome happen. E.g. Endure Harm, Endure Stress.
  • Envision two negative outcomes. Rate one as 'likely', and Ask the Oracle using the yes/no table. On a 'yes', make that outcome happen. Otherwise, make it the other.
  • Roll on the following table. If you have difficulty interpreting the result to fit the current situation, roll again.
moves.notes: The universal consequence move. When another move tells you to Pay the Price, choose how to determine the outcome: pick the obvious result, use the oracle to decide between two options, or roll on the table below.

table.value: Roll again and apply that result but make it worse. If you roll this result yet again, think of something dreadful that changes the course of your quest (Ask the Oracle if unsure) and make it happen.
A person or community you trusted loses faith in you, or acts against you.
A person or community you care about is exposed to danger.
You are separated from something or someone.
Your action has an unintended effect.
Something of value is lost or destroyed.
A new danger or foe is revealed.
It causes a delay or puts you at a disadvantage.
It is harmful. Endure Harm.
It is stressful. Endure Stress.
A surprising development complicates your quest.
It forces you to act against your best intentions.
A friend, companion, or ally is put in harm's way (or you are, if alone).
Roll twice more on this table. Both results occur. If they are the same result, make it worse.
